Describe yourself:
Dear Captain, Crewmember and Recruiter,

My name is Herman Bosman, I am a young, energetic, sporty, and hardworking 22-year-old from Pretoria, South Africa. 
I am an individual shaped by my dedication, dreams, and passions for life – which all have led me to become a Deckhand, but more specifically a licensed skipper… Additionally, I have been sailing since the young age of 13, together with my father. I consider myself to behave, and act on high ethical and moral grounds - practices and values showcasing 
humility, respect, honesty, tolerance, determination, perseverance, and dedication in all my given duties. All of which, I have reflected in my past leadership roles – titles including St. Alban’s College (School) “Prefect”, “Deputy-head-ofhouse”, and the University of Pretoria Youth Choir’s “head boy”. These personal character traits have given me immense 
responsibilities, and opportunities in the past, and I hope to acquire more throughout my career in the yachting industy. 

Additionally, these previous leadership roles involved staying committed to the group – thus, forgoing on a lot of my 
individual wants, and needs. However, I am grateful for all the learning opportunities I was granted.
Recently, I studied BA Intercultural Management in Austria, which I studied for 2 years however, after a lot of 
contemplation, I chose to follow in my aspirations of becoming a seafarer as my occupation. Something I am proud to be a part of. Since I started my career in the yachting industry, I have completed various qualifications, licenses, and courses. 

Furthermore, I, have also taken part in an internship to develop my knowledge on chase boats, and engine servicing. 
Specifically, on the servicing and repairs of outboard engines, which was done through my local boating company, 
“Motor king and Marine” (based in Pretoria, South Africa).
Regarding my deck qualifications, for both Motor and Sailing vessels: I hold various Royal Yachting Association (RYA), South African Maritime Safety Authority (SAMSA), and South African Sailing (SAS) licenses and accreditations. My current highest license being the RYA/MCA Day Skipper License, which allows me to skipper a sailing vessel under 200 tons, with a limitation of 15nm from the coast during light hours. In addition, I am proficient in washdowns, mooring, 
docking, anchoring, varnishing, polishing stainless-steel, VHF/SRC, and filling diving cylinders with “Compressed 
Breathing Air” *(CMAS Compressor Operator License), etc. 

My Hobbies include trail running, mountain biking, hiking, camping, snorkelling, and any other outdoor sports. 

Additionally, I can do bicycle repairs such as bleeding of hydraulic brakes, tubeless repairs, rim tape repair, spokes 
adjustments, gear adjustments, etc. 
Thus, I would like to put myself forward as a candidate for the given position on your vessel. I know, and believe that my 
current standards of skills, and qualifications would suit your needs and expectations – as asked for.

In conclusion, I do hope you consider my application.

Sincerely,
Herman Bosman

Antonio
